title: Hospice vs. Palliative Care: Understanding the Difference
summary: These two terms are often confused, but they apply at different points and don't require the same eligibility.
author: HarborCare Circle Editorial
seoTitle: Hospice vs. Palliative Care: Understanding the Difference | HarborCare Circle
metaDescription: These two terms are often confused, but they apply at different points and don't require the same eligibility.
whatYouNeedToKnow: Palliative care can start alongside curative treatment, at any stage of a serious illness.
Hospice specifically involves stepping away from curative treatment, generally with a six-month-or-less prognosis.
Both focus on comfort, but palliative care has a much broader window of eligibility.

paragraphs: Palliative care focuses on comfort and quality of life and can be provided at any stage of a serious illness, even alongside treatments aimed at curing or managing the disease — there's no requirement to stop other treatment to receive it.

Hospice, by contrast, specifically involves a shift away from curative treatment toward comfort-focused care, generally once a doctor estimates a prognosis of six months or less. Both share a comfort-centered philosophy, but they apply at different points and under different conditions.

nextSteps: Ask the care team whether palliative care might help now, regardless of prognosis.
Understand that palliative care doesn't require giving up other treatment.
Revisit the hospice conversation later if the situation progresses.
disclaimer: General information — the treating medical team can clarify which option fits the current situation.

sources.name: What Are Palliative Care and Hospice Care?
